title: helianthemum croceum subsp. stoechadifolium
intro: Helianthemum croceum subsp. stoechadifolium, also known as Rockrose or Stoechadifolium, is a plant species belonging to the family Cistaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum croceum subsp. stoechadifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Helianthemum croceum subsp. stoechadifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
